parsed_review.summary: CP00-502 is a descriptor-only governance-core pack (RP16.P06.M05.S17-RP16.P06.M06.S04, risk A, 10 units) that faithfully repeats the validated CP00-480..CP00-501 pattern. Pack binding is consistent across registry.js (GOVERNANCE_CORE_CP502_PACK_BINDING), service.js (descriptor/case-set), validators.js (coverage + slice validation), the governance-core-contract.json projection (current_pack and p06_permission_fixture_slice_descriptor), closeout-pack-plan, next-pack-queue, README, test/model.test.js, and the pack manifest. Deliverable distribution (security_audit 3 / test 4 / implementation 3), micro-phase row counts (RP16.P06.M05=6, RP16.P06.M06=4), and all 10 planned row titles match the plan included_units exactly. Every descriptor row is descriptor_only=true, runtime_execution=false, customer_safe_errors_only=true, raw_payload_included=false; the no-write attestation pins all governance/DLP/retention/legal-hold runtime, state writes, audit writes, Hermes runtime receipt, and real-data loading to false. The production_ready claim is correctly NOT overclaimed (production_ready=false, runtime_ready=false, flag governance_core_p06_permission_fixture_slice_descriptor_verified); the Hermes production_ready_candidate=true is a coverage/slice validity candidate distinct from production readiness. Authority boundary holds: claude_is_final_approval=false, claims_enterprise_trust_from_local_validation=false, human final approval required; Claude remains a read-only reviewer. Hermes patterns are confined to deterministic evidence-packet/receipt metadata, not product runtime. command-evidence.json records all implementation/plan/repo-wide validation commands at exit 0, and the readiness auth-preflight passed (exit 0, 1436-byte valid JSON, no auth failure, no 0-byte/malformed/tool-call-shaped output). The post-review artifacts (claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, construction-inspection.json) are expectedly absent at review time and produced downstream in the hardening sequence, matching the CP00-501 precedent. Unrelated dirty files (progress-control-room UI, workbook docs) are correctly classified outside pack scope; index.js is unmodified because it wildcard re-exports. No P0/P1/P2/P3 findings. This review is independent and read-only and does not close the enterprise trust claim; human final approval remains required.
